Console enter commands (code) R interpret.give try, place cursor Console window clicking . see greater sign > (called “prompt”) blinking cursor next .Type number 2 press enter key. Pressing enter like asking R question. R evaluate code typed (yes, ’s really computer code!) print output, answer question, screen like :
Congratulations, just wrote first computer program!2 answer question [1] indicates answer one part. part can ignore number brackets.rest tutorial, use following notation indicate code type console, output look like:Note examples, show prompt add two hash marks #> indicate lines output.Tip ever try type something console nothing happens, may pane RStudio’s attention. Click Console pane make sure blinking cursor showing.

text: may also noticed ran assignment command, Environment pane changed. heading: 2.3.6 Functions
text: mathematical operations operator. must use known function. example, take square root:function consists name function followed parentheses, list arguments inside parentheses. functions take one argument, take , take none .commonly used function c(), combine function. combines multiple values ’s known vector:functions take vectors arguments. example, length() function returns number elements, length, vector object.Useful mathematical functions include:can even nest functions. example , c() function evaluated first, value used argument length function.Combining several features together, can calculate standard error mean (SEM) confidence interval around mean set numbers:calculated SEM can use estimate upper lower limits 95% confidence interval like :
code:
sqrt(9)
#> [1] 3
k <- c(1, 2, 10, 4.7, 5.0)
k
#> [1]  1.0  2.0 10.0  4.7  5.0
length(k)
#> [1] 5
median(k)  # median value
#> [1] 4.7
mean(k)    # mean value
#> [1] 4.54
sd(k)      # standard deviation
#> [1] 3.501143
length(c(1, 2, 10, 4.7, 5.0)) # nested function
#> [1] 5
n <- length(k) # sample size
n
#> [1] 5
sem <- sd(k) / sqrt(n) # standard error of the mean
sem
#> [1] 1.565759
mean(k) + 1.96 * sem  # upper limit
#> [1] 7.608887
mean(k) - 1.96 * sem  # lower limit
#> [1] 1.471113
c(mean(k) + 1.96 * sem, mean(k) - 1.96 * sem)
#> [1] 7.608887 1.471113

heading: 2.4.2.2 What’s in an R Markdown document?
text: three main components R Markdown document:YAML headerA YAML headerNarrative textNarrative textChunks R code R run “knit” document create lab report. Usually code chunks generate text output like appears Console tab run code, figures tables graphs.Chunks R code R run “knit” document create lab report. Usually code chunks generate text output like appears Console tab run code, figures tables graphs.Let’s look component depth.

heading: 2.4.2.2.1 YAML header
text: notice header information top, two sets three dashes, looks like :knit document, R use title page title put author date next line.Notice instead writing date text, R code. called inline R code R Markdown document. want insert something generated R text, can use kind code. Inline code start backtick letter r `r end another backtick `. another example inline code:One plus one 2When R knits appear asOne plus one 2

author: "___"
date: "2021-01-20"
output: github_document
